verses.text: After the death of Moses, the LORD spake unto Joshua: Moses my servant is dead; now arise, go over this Jordan. Every place the sole of your foot shall tread upon, that have I given unto you. From the wilderness and Lebanon unto the Euphrates, unto the great sea — shall be your coast. As I was with Moses, so I will be with thee: I will not fail thee, nor forsake thee. Guidance: The text wastes no time. Moses is named dead in the same verse Joshua is named commander. God transfers the same promise — 'I will be with thee.' Mourning the predecessor and obeying the next call are simultaneous, not sequential. The land is still given; the river is still ahead.

Be strong and of a good courage: for unto this people shalt thou divide the land. Only be thou strong and very courageous, that thou mayest observe to do all the law: turn not to the right hand or to the left. This book of the law shall not depart out of thy mouth; thou shalt meditate therein day and night: for then thou shalt make thy way prosperous, then thou shalt have good success. Be strong, be not afraid: for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest. Guidance: Three times 'be strong and of a good courage' in four verses. Courage is the recurring command of the chapter. And the source: meditate on the book day and night. The new leader's first work is not strategy but scripture. The road is paved by what fills your mouth.

Joshua commanded the officers: Pass through the host, command the people, Prepare you victuals; for within three days ye shall pass over this Jordan, to go in to possess the land. Guidance: Three days from sermon to river. Joshua hears the charge and immediately turns to logistics — bake bread, pack, count yourselves. Faith expressed in groceries. The promised land is invaded not by miracle alone but by people who packed a lunch.

Joshua spake unto the Reubenites, Gadites, half tribe of Manasseh: Remember the word Moses commanded — the LORD hath given you rest, given you this land. Your wives, little ones, cattle, shall remain in the land Moses gave you east of Jordan; ye shall pass before your brethren armed, help them; until the LORD have given your brethren rest. Then ye shall return unto the land of your possession. Guidance: The 2.5 tribes are reminded of their pledge from Numbers 32. Families east, swords west. Joshua starts by holding everyone accountable to what they promised the dying prophet. Memory binds a community more than orders do.

They answered Joshua: All that thou commandest us we will do, whithersoever thou sendest us we will go. According as we hearkened unto Moses, so will we hearken unto thee: only the LORD thy God be with thee, as He was with Moses. Whosoever rebels against thy commandment shall be put to death: only be strong and of a good courage. Guidance: The people give Joshua what he didn't dare ask for: their loyalty as a vote. 'As we hearkened to Moses, so to thee' — a public transition of allegiance. And they echo God's command back to him: be strong, be courageous. The new leader is held up by both heaven and the camp.
